tool_args.label: MTA Real Estate Department
tool_args.raw_text: MTA REAL ESTATE DEPARTMENT REAL PROPERTY DISPOSITION GUIDELINES

payload.description: The MTA Real Estate Department is comprised of two core groups – Transactions & Operations (including Facilities Management) ("TOM" or "RED") and Transit-Oriented Development ("TOD"). Responsible for managing all real estate operational, transactional and development-related matters and utility franchise agreements involving the disposition of real property or interests in real property.

evidence_refs.source_quote: METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION AUTHORITY REAL ESTATE DEPARTMENT REAL PROPERTY DISPOSITION GUIDELINES

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

tool_args.label: Scope of Real Property Disposition Guidelines
tool_args.raw_text: These Guidelines apply only to dispositions of real property under Sections 2895-2897 of the Public Authorities Law ("PAL"), including leasing-out or sale of real property and grants of interests in real property such as easements and leasehold interests, owned or leased and controlled by the MTA or other MTA Agency.

payload.claim_text: These Guidelines apply only to dispositions of real property under Sections 2895-2897 of the Public Authorities Law (PAL), including leasing-out or sale of real property and grants of interests in real property such as easements and leasehold interests, owned or leased and controlled by the MTA or other MTA Agency.
payload.subject: All-Agency Real Property Disposition Guidelines

evidence_refs.source_quote: These Guidelines apply only to dispositions of real property under Sections 2895-2897 of the Public Authorities Law

tool_args.label: Purpose of Personal Property Disposal Guidelines
tool_args.raw_text: The purpose of these guidelines is to: 1. Ensure that disposal of MTA personal property is undertaken in compliance with governing laws; 2. Define the means for identifying obsolete, unneeded, or otherwise outdated personal property; 3. Provide cost-savings to the MTA by outlining a procedure for the transfer of surplus personal property; 4. Recover value from surplus personal property through its sale; and 5. Minimize disposal and storage costs by providing means for the donation of surplus personal property with negligible resale value.

payload.claim_text: The purpose of these guidelines is to: 1. Ensure that disposal of MTA personal property is undertaken in compliance with governing laws; 2. Define the means for identifying obsolete, unneeded, or otherwise outdated personal property, including but not limited to furniture, equipment, computer equipment, and automobiles; 3. Provide cost-savings to the MTA by outlining a procedure for the transfer of surplus personal property; 4. Recover value from surplus personal property through its sale, either for reuse or for scrap; and 5. Minimize disposal and storage costs by providing means for the donation of surplus personal property with negligible resale value.
payload.subject: All-Agency Guidelines for the Disposal of Personal Property

evidence_refs.source_quote: The purpose of these guidelines is to:

tool_args.label: MTA Real Estate Governance Structure
tool_args.raw_text: MTA's Chief Administrative Officer (the "CAO") oversees the RED/TOM functions. The Chief Real Estate Transactions and Operations Officer ("Managing Director, TOM")—reporting to the CAO, has primary day-to-day responsibility for managing all TOM functions.

payload.claim_text: MTA's Chief Administrative Officer (the "CAO") oversees the RED's RED/TOM functions. The Chief Real Estate Transactions and Operations Officer ("Managing Director, TOM")—reporting to the CAO, has primary day-to-day responsibility for managing all TOM functions, with direct oversight for, among others, the Director, Transaction Management ("First Deputy, TOM"), Director, Grand Central Retail Leasing and Management, Director, Tenant Management, Director, Operations, Director, Project Management and Director, Finance & Administration.
payload.subject: MTA Real Estate Governance

tool_args.label: MTA Transit-Oriented Development Governance
tool_args.raw_text: The Senior Vice President, Transit-Oriented Development ("Managing Director, TOD")—reporting to the Chief Development Officer (the "CDO") through the Deputy CDO, Planning—has primary day-to-day responsibility for managing all TOD functions.

payload.claim_text: The Senior Vice President, Transit-Oriented Development ("Managing Director, TOD")—reporting to the Chief Development Officer (the "CDO") through the Deputy CDO, Planning—has primary day-to-day responsibility for managing all TOD functions with direct oversight for, among others, the Vice President, Planning, Vice President, Transactions and Director, TOD Program Management.
payload.subject: MTA Transit-Oriented Development Governance

tool_args.label: MTA Real Estate Transactions & Operations (TOM/RED)
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Real Estate comprises two core groups – (i) Transactions & Operations (including Facilities Management) ("TOM" or "RED") and (ii) Transit-Oriented Development ("TOD")

payload.description: The TOM group is responsible for all real estate-related transactional matters (other than development and major capital projects) for the MTA Agencies (including acquisitions, dispositions, and leasing of MTA Property), tenant management, and management and operations of MTA Facilities. Also referred to as RED.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Real Estate comprises two core groups – (i) Transactions & Operations (including Facilities Management) ("TOM" or "RED")

tool_args.label: MTA Transit-Oriented Development (TOD)
tool_args.raw_text: Transit-Oriented Development ("TOD") group of MTA C&D

payload.description: The TOD group is responsible for all real estate-related development matters, whether by an MTA Agency, a government or other public or quasi-public entity and/or a private entity, which affects or involves any existing or to-be acquired MTA Property and/or any existing or proposed transit-related facility or amenity, including acquisitions and dispositions of real property or interests in real property for long-term revenue generation and transportation facilities; major capital projects; co-development arrangements; and public-private partnerships.
